items.question: She's ___ engineer at a tech company.
explanations: Correct! Jobs take a/an; 'engineer' has a vowel sound: 'an engineer'.
'One engineer' wrongly stresses the number for a job.
'Engineer' has a vowel sound, so 'an', not 'a'.
'The one' doesn't fit before a job.

items.question: I have ___ brother and two sisters. (stressing the number)
explanations: Correct! Contrast with 'two sisters' makes the number matter: 'one brother'.
'A brother' is possible but doesn't stress the number.
'An' is for vowel sounds and doesn't stress number here.
'The one brother' changes the meaning.

items.question: Can I have ___ coffee, please? (just ordering)
explanations: Correct! Naming a drink: 'a coffee'.
'One only' is unnatural for a simple order.
'The one' changes the meaning.
'Coffee' has a consonant sound, so 'a', not 'an'.

items.question: ___ day I'll visit Japan.
explanations: Correct! Fixed phrase: 'One day I'll…'.
'A day' changes the meaning to a single day's length.
'An day' is wrong.
'The one day' changes the meaning.

items.question: There are two jackets. I'll take the black ___ .
explanations: Correct! 'One' replaces the noun: 'the black one'.
'A' can't be used as a pronoun here.
'An' can't be used as a pronoun here.
'The black it' is not correct.

items.question: He's ___ of my best friends.
explanations: Correct! 'One of my best friends' — the fixed pattern.
'A of my friends' is not correct.
'An of my friends' is not correct.
'The a' is not correct.

items.question: Only ___ student passed the exam — everyone else failed.
explanations: Correct! Stressing the number: 'only one student'.
'A student' doesn't stress 'only one'.
'An student' is wrong.
'The a' is not correct.

items.question: I saw ___ amazing film last night.
explanations: Correct! Naming it; 'amazing' has a vowel sound: 'an amazing film'.
'One amazing film' oddly stresses the number.
'Amazing' has a vowel sound, so 'an', not 'a'.
'The one' changes the meaning.

items.question: Correct this: 'My mother is one doctor.'
options: My mother is the one doctor.
explanations: Correct! Jobs take a/an: 'a doctor'.
'One doctor' wrongly stresses the number for a job.
'Doctor' has a consonant sound, so 'a', not 'an'.
'The one doctor' changes the meaning.

items.question: When do you use 'one' instead of 'a/an'?
options: When the exact number matters, or as a pronoun.
explanations: Correct! 'One' stresses number, or replaces a noun.
Most singular nouns just take a/an.
Jobs take a/an, not 'one'.
'One' isn't limited to questions.
